title: pollinia articulata var. concinna
side_effects: Pollinia articulata var. concinna can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. If ingested, it can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic properties. Furthermore, excessive exposure to its sap can lead to inflammation and blistering on the skin, potentially resulting in scarring. In severe cases, improper handling of the plant can trigger anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction.
